source.title: PDD Holdings Q4 2025 Earnings Call - Investment Cycle & 2026 Outlook

extractions.claim: Management signaled supply chain investment as 'greatest conviction' for 2026, with explicit warning of financial performance impact
extractions.evidence: Co-CEO Jiazhen Zhao: '2026 marks the beginning of our journey into the next decade. Going into this new chapter, supply chain investment is where we will place our greatest conviction. We are committed to dedicating significant resources, with an all-in mindset, to drive lasting benefits to the entire ecosystem.' VP Finance Jun Liu: 'These investments are firm and long-term, and will inevitably affect our financial performance.'

verification.verifier_notes: From Q4 2025 earnings call press release and transcript summaries

extractions.claim: RMB 100B merchant support program launched in 2025 caused operating profit to decline 13% YoY; program will continue and expand in 2026
extractions.evidence: The 'Hundred Billion Support Program' was launched in early 2025. Key components: RMB 10B fee reduction program, logistics support initiative, new quality merchant support. Impact: FY2025 operating profit declined to RMB 94.6B from RMB 108.4B (-13%). Zhao described 2025 as 'the year in which we made our largest investment in high-quality development.' Program will continue in 2026.

verification.verifier_notes: From earnings call and press release. Unclear how much of the RMB 100B has been spent vs committed.

extractions.claim: Q4 2025 operating profit grew +8.3% YoY, suggesting the worst of margin compression may be over — or Q4 was seasonally strong
extractions.evidence: Q4 2025 operating profit RMB 27.7B vs Q4 2024 RMB 25.6B (+8.3%). Quarterly operating profit trajectory in 2025: Q1 RMB 16.1B (-38% YoY), Q2 RMB 25.8B (-21% YoY), Q3 RMB 25.0B (+2% YoY), Q4 RMB 27.7B (+8.3% YoY). Clear improving trend through the year.

verification.verifier_notes: Q1-Q3 from respective quarterly earnings releases. Q4 from today's release. Trajectory shows sequential improvement.

extractions.claim: PDD has NO fintech business, unlike Alibaba (Ant Group) and JD.com (JD Finance) — this is a notable absence given its cash pile and merchant base
extractions.evidence: PDD Holdings does not operate a publicly disclosed fintech platform comparable to Alibaba's Ant Group or JD.com's JD Finance. No merchant lending, consumer credit, or digital banking products are disclosed. Transaction services revenue appears limited to payment processing and logistics fees. This means PDD's ~RMB 600B in liquid assets earn interest income but do not support a lending business that could generate much higher returns.

verification.verifier_notes: Based on absence of fintech disclosures in all public filings and earnings materials. PDD has payment processing capabilities (Duoduo Pay) but does not appear to have expanded into lending or credit.
